name: deny-loop-burst-dedup
doc: Fan-out burst dedup end-to-end (v0.1.55). Every deployed plugin registers its own PreToolUse hook, so ONE tool event spawns ~11 sibling binaries that each recordDeny the SAME (hash+session_id) within milliseconds — pre-fix this inflated the counter by ~11 ([REPEAT] '#9' on the FIRST real try). This scenario proves the collapse THROUGH the real binary: step 1 = first deny (count 1); step 2 fired IMMEDIATELY (no delay) simulates a sibling hook of the same event — folded into #1 by BURST_DEDUP_MS (src/runtime/burst-window.ts, deny-loop-store.ts recordDeny), so it stays a normal deny, NOT a [REPEAT]; step 3, spaced past the window (delayMs 2100), is the FIRST genuine retry → count 2, NOT 3. Asserting `Identical attempt #2` on step 3 is the discriminator: without the dedup step 2 would have bumped to 2 and step 3 to 3 (`#3`). Sidecar deny-loop.json persists across spawns in dirname(trackFile) (HOME+cwd stable).

steps.doc: Sibling hook of the SAME event, fired immediately (<2s) → folded into #1, still a NORMAL deny (no [REPEAT] title). This is the ×11 amplification being collapsed to ×1.
First GENUINE retry, spaced past the burst window → count 2 (NOT 3, which is exactly what proves step 2 did not re-increment). enrichRepeatDeny → `Identical attempt #2`.
